zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret verch Thomas於1444年出生在英國威爾斯卡馬特史爾曼諾爾代洛時，她的父親Thomas ap Gruffydd Earl of Cardiff19歲，母親Elsbeth  Elisabeth deBourgogne ap Gruffudd  Griffith19歲。她於大約1454年與Sir Richard "Hîr" Herbert在英國威爾斯蒙姆爾可爾德布盧可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1499年3月17日在威爾士蒙姆爾阿加文尼享年55歲，埋葬在威爾士蒙姆爾阿加文尼。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret verch Thomas于1444年出生在英国威尔斯卡馬特史爾曼諾爾代洛时，她的父亲Thomas ap Gruffydd Earl of Cardiff19岁，母亲Elsbeth  Elisabeth deBourgogne ap Gruffudd  Griffith19岁。她于大约1454年与Sir Richard "Hîr" Herbert在英国威尔斯蒙姆爾可爾德布盧可结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1499年3月17日在威尔士蒙姆爾阿加文尼享年55岁，埋葬在威尔士蒙姆爾阿加文尼。

nameMeanings.name: verch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Orlo.
German: variant of   Ferch  .

variants.name: Ferch
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Breton, German, Dutch, Flemish, Danish, Greek, West Indian (mainly Haiti and Jamaica), and African (mainly Tanzania and Nigeria): from the personal name Thomas, of Biblical (New Testament) origin, from Aramaic t’ōm’a, a byname meaning ‘twin’. It was borne by one of the disciples of Christ, known for his scepticism about Christ's resurrection (John 20:24–29). The Th- spelling is organic, the initial letter of the name in the Greek New Testament being a theta. The English pronunciation as t rather than th- is the result of French influence from an early date. In Britain, the surname is widely distributed throughout the country, but especially common in Wales and Cornwall. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages (e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Toma and Tuma , Albanian Toma and Thoma , and Slavic surnames listed in 3 below), and their patronymics and other derivatives (e.g. Polish Tomaszewski and Slovenian Tomažič; see Tomazic ). In France, this surname is most common in the Vosges and Brittany. The name Thomas is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Machan , Mammen , and Oommen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name Thomas (see 1 above) as a surname.

Germanized or Americanized form of Polish Tomas , Tomasz, and Tomaś, Sorbian Tomaš (see also 4 below), Croatian Tomaš and Tomas , Slovenian Tomaš and Tomaž, Czech and Slovak Tomáš, all meaning ‘Thomas’.

variants.name: Maas
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: from a short form of the personal name   Thomas  .

variants.name: Mashak
meanings: Americanized form of Polish Maszak: patronymic from a short form of the personal name Tomasz (see Thomas ) or possibly of a personal name beginning with Ma-. The surname Maszak is very rare in Poland.
In some cases also an Americanized form of Czech Mašek (see Masek ). Compare Mashek .

variants.name: Maschke
meanings: German: from a Slavic-influenced (see below) pet form of the personal names   Thomas   (compare   Maas  ) or   Matthias  .
Germanized form of Sorbian Mašk, Maška, and Maško: from a pet form of the personal name Domaš or Tomaš (see Thomas 3, 4), or of any name beginning with Ma-.
Germanized form of Czech Mašek (see Masek ) and Polish Maszek, both cognates of 2 above.

variants.name: Toma
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Kamal, Nabil, Salem, Kamil, Safa, Samir, Adil, Adnan, Aziz, Bahjat, Basem, Bashar.  Japanese Hiroshi, Takeo, Hideo, Isako, Izumi, Junichi, Kenji, Kiyoko, Masakazu, Rikuo, Sachi, Shigeo.
Romanian and Croatian: from the personal name Toma, an equivalent of Thomas . Compare Tomas .

Italian, French, Polish, Slovak, and Hungarian: from a short, pet, or dialect form of a personal name equivalent to Thomas (Italian Tommaso, French Thomas, Polish Tomasz, Slovak Tomáš, Hungarian Tamás).

variants.name: Mask
meanings: English: unexplained.
German: from a pet form of the personal name   Thomas  .

variants.name: Masino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Ciro, Larraine.
Italian: from a short form of Tommasino, a pet form of thepersonal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

variants.name: Mascio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Carlo, Gino, Luigi, Paolo, Salvatore.
Italian:
(southern): from a variant of Maso, a short form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ). Alternatively, perhaps from an Italianized form of the Albanian equivalent Mashi.

variants.name: Masel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt.
German: from a pet form of   Thomas  .

variants.name: Masi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Mario, Vito, Carlo, Antonio, Alfonso, Armando, Filomena, Gerardo, Giovanni, Salvatore, Alfonse, Alfonsina, Amato, Anello, Canio, Emilio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Maso, a short form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

variants.name: Masiello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Rocco, Angelo, Carmine, Franco, Salvatore, Antonio, Carmino, Domenic, Domenica, Francesco, Mauro.
Italian (mainly Naples): from a pet form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret verch Thomas was born in 1444, in Manordeilo, Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas ap Gruffydd Earl of Cardiff, was 19 and her mother, Elsbeth  Elisabeth deBourgogne ap Gruffudd  Griffith, was 19. She married Sir Richard "Hîr" Herbert about 1454, in Coldbrook, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 17 March 1499, in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales, at the age of 55, and was buried in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret verch Thomas est née en 1444, à Manordeilo, Carmarthenshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas ap Gruffydd Earl of Cardiff, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Elsbeth  Elisabeth deBourgogne ap Gruffudd  Griffith, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Richard "Hîr" Herbert environ 1454, à Coldbrook, Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 17 mars 1499, à Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Galles, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterrée à Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Galles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Margaret verch Thomas 1444 in Manordeilo, Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas ap Gruffydd Earl of Cardiff 19 und ihre Mutter Elsbeth  Elisabeth deBourgogne ap Gruffudd  Griffith 19. Sie hat um 1454 in Coldbrook, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom Sir Richard "Hîr" Herbert geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 17. März 1499 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales und wurde in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret verch Thomas è nata il nel 1444 nel Manordeilo, Carmarthenshire, Galles, Regno Unito, sua padre, Thomas ap Gruffydd Earl of Cardiff, aveva 19 anni e sua madre, Elsbeth  Elisabeth deBourgogne ap Gruffudd  Griffith, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato Sir Richard "Hîr" Herbert circa 1454, nel Coldbrook, Monmouthshire, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 17 marzo 1499, nel Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Galles, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolta a Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Galles.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret verch Thomasが1444年にイギリスウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアマノーデイロで生まれた時，父Thomas ap Gruffydd Earl of Cardiffは19歳，母Elsbeth  Elisabeth deBourgogne ap Gruffudd  Griffithは19歳であった。約1454年にイギリスウェールズモンモウスシュアイアコルドブルークでSir Richard "Hîr" Herbertと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1499年3月17日にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアアバーガベニーで55歳で死去し，ウェールズモンモウスシュアイアアバーガベニーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret verch Thomas(이)가 1444년 영국 웨일스 카마테실르 먼르데이로에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas ap Gruffydd Earl of Cardiff(은)는 19세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elsbeth  Elisabeth deBourgogne ap Gruffudd  Griffith(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 대략 1454년 영국 웨일스 몬모우스실르 콜드브룩에서 Sir Richard "Hîr" Herbert(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1499년 3월 17일 웨일스 몬모우스실르 압르가븐이에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 웨일스 몬모우스실르 압르가븐이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret verch Thomas nasceu em 1444, em Manordeilo, Carmarthenshire, Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas ap Gruffydd Earl of Cardiff, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Elsbeth  Elisabeth deBourgogne ap Gruffudd  Griffith, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Richard "Hîr" Herbert aproximadamente 1454, em Coldbrook, Monmouthshire, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 17 de março de 1499, em Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Gales, com 55 anos, e foi sepultada em Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Gales.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret verch Thomas родилась в 1444 г. в местности манордилоу, кармартеншир, Уэльс, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas ap Gruffydd Earl of Cardiff было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Elsbeth  Elisabeth deBourgogne ap Gruffudd  Griffith, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard "Hîr" Herbert около 1454 г. в местности колдбрук, монмоутшир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 17 марта 1499 г. в местности абиргавенни, монмоутшир, Уэльс в возрасте 55 лет и была похоронена в местности абиргавенни, монмоутшир, Уэльс.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Margaret verch Thomas nació en 1444, en Manordeilo, Carmarthenshire, Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Thomas ap Gruffydd Earl of Cardiff, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Elsbeth  Elisabeth deBourgogne ap Gruffudd  Griffith, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Sir Richard "Hîr" Herbert aproximadamente 1454, en Coldbrook, Monmouthshire, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 17 de marzo de 1499, en Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Gales, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultada en Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Gales.
